tavily.answer: In Yemen, the United Nations’ food agencies have warned that acute hunger is set to worsen across 13 global hotspots, including Yemen, due to conflict, funding shortages, and climate shocks pushing millions closer to famine. The Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) and the World Food Program (WFP) highlighted that around 266 million people are already facing high levels of acute food insecurity. Meanwhile, a Houthi-controlled court in Yemen sentenced 19 people to death for aiding the Saudi-led coalition. Additionally, Yemen’s government and Houthis agreed to free 1,600 detainees in the largest swap of the 11-year war, which is expected to facilitate a more inclusive political process for peace.
